top_categories.workout_context: 129ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Wasatch All-Road's Mountain gravel, High altitude forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 82 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Wasatch All-Road's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
At Wasatch All-Road, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
10,560ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Wasatch All-Road. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 82, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
10,560ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 82 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Wasatch All-Road, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Low-cadence force work for 10,560ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 82 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Wasatch All-Road’s 82 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Wasatch All-Road with 10,560ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. High altitude (5,600-9,000+ feet); Sun exposure at altitude.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Wasatch All-Road requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on wasatch range mountain gravel to build confidence. Expect: mountain gravel, high altitude, wasatch range.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 82 miles of wasatch range mountain gravel in Heber City, Utah.
